$schema_doc: Each term: { term: string to embed; lang: 'en'|'la'; area: one of the listed taxonomies; short: 3-15 word definition shown in tooltip }.

terms.short: Guilty mind; the mental element of a crime.
Guilty act; the physical element of a crime.
Conscious aim or purpose to bring about a result.
Mental state requiring purpose to achieve a particular result.
Awareness of acting; no specific purpose required.
Intent toward one target carried over to an unintended victim.
Conscious disregard of a substantial unjustifiable risk.
Failure to perceive a substantial risk a reasonable person would see.
Deliberate avoidance of knowledge to escape liability.
Serious crime typically punishable by more than one year imprisonment.
Less serious crime, lower maximum sentence.
Levying war against the state or aiding its enemies.
Unlawful killing with malice aforethought.
Unlawful killing without malice; voluntary or involuntary.
Killing during commission of a dangerous felony.
Forming intent to kill before the act.
Intent to kill or cause grievous harm; element of murder.
Taking property by force or threat from a person.
Unlawful entry of a building intending to commit a crime.
Trespassory taking and carrying away of another's property.
Conversion of property by one entrusted with it.
Deception causing loss to another for the deceiver's gain.
Falsifying a document with intent to defraud.
Malicious burning of a building or property.
Threat or attempt to inflict imminent harmful contact.
Unlawful intentional harmful or offensive contact.
Unlawful confinement and movement of a person.
Unlawful restriction of another's freedom of movement.
Non-consensual sexual penetration.
Non-consensual sexual contact.
Offering something of value to influence official conduct.
Obtaining property through threats or coercion.
Operating a criminal enterprise across coordinated activity.
Concealing illicit origin of funds through transactions.
Wilfully giving false testimony under oath.
Interfering with the administration of legal proceedings.
Disobedience or disrespect of judicial authority.
Agreement between two or more to commit an unlawful act.
Substantial step toward a crime that is not completed.
Inducing another to commit a crime.
Liability for aiding or encouraging another's crime.
Assisting or facilitating commission of a crime.
Justified force to repel imminent unlawful attack.
Justified force to protect another from imminent attack.
Justification for breaking the law to avoid greater harm.
Excuse based on coercion by serious threat.
Defence based on inducement by law enforcement.
Excuse based on mental incapacity at the time of the offence.
Reduced mental ability negating specific intent.
Honest factual misunderstanding negating mens rea.
Ignorance of law; rarely a defence.
Prohibition of being tried twice for the same offence.
Defendant deemed innocent until proven guilty.
Retroactive criminal law; constitutionally prohibited.
Legislative declaration of guilt; constitutionally prohibited.
Punishment justified by desert for past wrong.
Punishment justified by prevention of future offences.
Punishment aimed at reforming the offender.
Confinement to prevent further offending.
Reasonable basis to believe a crime occurred or evidence exists.
Articulable basis for brief investigative stop.
Judicial authorisation to search a specific place.
Judicial authorisation to take a person into custody.
Bar on use of unconstitutionally obtained evidence.
Evidence derived from an illegal source is also excluded.
Evidence kept despite warrant defect if police acted reasonably.
Notice of rights required before custodial interrogation.
Questioning while a person's freedom is significantly restricted.
Constitutional right to legal representation.
Compelled testimony against oneself; protected against.
Right to be tried without undue delay.
Right to face and cross-examine accusers.
Right to subpoena defence witnesses.
Jury selection examination by counsel and the court.
Removal of a juror without stated reason.
Removal of a juror for stated bias or disqualification.
Objection to jury strikes based on race or gender.
Acquittal contrary to law and evidence by the jury.
Obligation to establish a fact in legal proceedings.
Degree of certainty required to establish a fact.
More likely than not; civil burden.
Highly probable; intermediate civil burden.
No reasonable doubt remains; criminal burden.
Security for appearance at trial in exchange for release.
Negotiated plea in exchange for sentencing concessions.
No-contest plea; not a denial but not a confession.
Imposition of punishment after conviction.
Statutorily required minimum sentence for an offence.
Punishment violating constitutional dignity standards.
Authority of a court to hear the type of case.
Authority of a court over the parties before it.
Federal authority over disputes between citizens of different states.
Federal authority over claims arising under federal law.
Authority over related claims forming the same case.
Geographic location where a case may be brought.
Discretionary dismissal in favour of a more appropriate forum.
Determining which jurisdiction's law applies.
Formal notification of a lawsuit to the defendant.
Initial pleading stating the plaintiff's claims.
Defendant's responsive pleading admitting or denying allegations.
Claim by defendant against plaintiff in same action.
Adding parties or claims to an existing action.
Suit brought by representatives on behalf of a group.
Pretrial process to obtain evidence from opposing parties.
Out-of-court sworn testimony recorded for later use.
Written question requiring written response under oath.
Decision without trial where no material fact is disputed.
Request to terminate a case for legal insufficiency.
Court ruling for a party when evidence permits no other outcome.
Matter already decided; bars relitigation.
Issue preclusion across different proceedings.
Stand by decided things; doctrine of binding precedent.
Prior judicial decision used as authority.
Reason for the decision; the binding rule of a case.
Said in passing; non-binding judicial observation.
Writ requiring justification for detention.
Discretionary appellate review by a higher court.
Order compelling a public official to perform a duty.
Friend of the court; non-party brief.
Proceeding involving only one party.
In chambers; conducted in private.
Action against a specific person.
Action against a thing rather than a person.
Standing; the right to bring an action.
Order requiring attendance or production of evidence.
Time limit within which a suit must be filed.
Equitable bar based on unreasonable delay.
Court order compelling or forbidding specific conduct.
Pre-trial order preserving status pending decision.
Tendency to make a material fact more or less probable.
Connection of evidence to a fact of consequence.
Out-of-court statement offered for its truth.
Statement describing event made while perceiving it.
Spontaneous statement under stress of a startling event.
Statement by someone believing death imminent, about its cause.
Out-of-court statement contrary to declarant's interest.
Hearsay exception for regularly kept business records.
Hearsay exception for public office records.
Evidence of a person's general traits, with restricted use.
Opinion from a qualified specialist, governed by Daubert/Frye.
Federal test for admissibility of scientific expert testimony.
General-acceptance test for scientific evidence in some states.
Original document required to prove its content.
Showing evidence is what its proponent claims.
Confidentiality protection for legal advice communications.
Protection of materials prepared in anticipation of litigation.
Confidentiality protection for marital communications.
Confidentiality protection for medical communications.
Right against compelled self-incrimination.
Court acceptance of indisputable facts without proof.
Inference required by law unless rebutted.
Presumption that may be overcome by contrary evidence.
Presumption not subject to contradiction.
Documented handling of physical evidence over time.
Manifested willingness to enter a bargain on stated terms.
Manifested assent to the terms of an offer.
Bargained-for exchange of value between parties.
Meeting of the minds; agreement on essential terms.
Manifested willingness to be legally bound.
Both parties mistaken about a basic assumption.
Mistake by only one party; rarely a basis for relief.
False statement inducing the contract.
Fraud about the nature or terms of the bargain.
Misrepresentation about the document being signed.
Improper threat depriving the victim of free will.
Unfair persuasion by one in a relationship of trust.
Legal ability to enter into a binding agreement.
Person under legal age; contracts often voidable.
Requirement that certain contracts be in writing.
Bar on prior agreements contradicting an integrated writing.
Standardised form contract offered on take-it-or-leave-it basis.
Contract terms so unfair as to be unenforceable.
Unfairness in the bargaining process.
Unfairness in the contract's substantive terms.
Doctrine voiding contracts contrary to societal interests.
Contract for an unlawful purpose; unenforceable.
Performance objectively impossible; excuses obligation.
Performance unreasonably difficult due to unforeseen events.
Excuse from performance when underlying purpose disappears.
Unforeseeable event excusing contractual performance.
Breach substantial enough to discharge the other party.
Performance with only trivial deviations from contract.
Clear refusal to perform before performance is due.
Court order requiring exact contract performance.
Unmaking of a contract; restoration of pre-contract status.
Benefit of the bargain; what was expected from performance.
Costs incurred in reliance on the promise.
Recovery of value conferred to prevent unjust enrichment.
Indirect losses foreseeable at contract formation.
Pre-estimated damages stipulated in the contract.
Duty to take reasonable steps to limit losses.
Promise enforceable when reliance makes breach unjust.
As much as deserved; recovery for services rendered.
Benefit retained without justification at another's expense.
Doctrine restricting enforcement to actual parties.
Non-party intended to benefit from a contract.
Transfer of contractual rights to another party.
Transfer of contractual duties to another.
Substitution of a new party or obligation for an old one.
Honesty and fair dealing in performance.
Agreements must be kept; binding force of contract.
Things thus standing; clause permitting change with circumstances.
Let the buyer beware.
Failure to exercise reasonable care, causing harm.
Legal obligation to take reasonable care to avoid harm.
Failure to meet the applicable standard of care.
Causal link between the defendant's act and the harm.
Harm would not have occurred without the conduct.
Legally sufficient cause; cuts off remote consequences.
Capacity of a reasonable person to anticipate harm.
Hypothetical prudent actor used as standard of conduct.
Liability without proof of fault or intent.
One party held liable for another's wrongful acts.
Employer liability for acts within scope of employment.
Plaintiff's own fault contributing to the harm.
Apportionment of fault between plaintiff and defendant.
Voluntary acceptance of known danger; defence to negligence.
Doctrine reviving claim despite contributory negligence.
The thing speaks for itself; inferred negligence.
Liability for harm caused by defective products.
Inherent product flaw making it unreasonably dangerous.
Departure from intended design in a particular unit.
Inadequate notice of a product's known dangers.
False statement injuring another's reputation.
Defamation in written or printed form.
Defamation in spoken form.
Knowledge of falsity or reckless disregard for truth.
Extreme conduct causing severe mental anguish.
Highly offensive intrusion into another's private affairs.
Public revelation of non-newsworthy private information.
Highly offensive misrepresentation of a person to the public.
Commercial use of another's name or image without consent.
Unauthorised physical entry onto another's property.
Intentional interference with another's personal property.
Substantial interference depriving owner of personal property.
Substantial unreasonable interference with use of land.
Unreasonable interference with a right common to the public.
To one who consents, no harm is done.
Land and things permanently attached to it.
Movable things; chattels.
Most complete ownership; potentially infinite duration.
Ownership subject to a condition that can terminate it.
Possessory interest measured by a life.
Future interest taking effect after a prior estate ends.
Future interest retained by the grantor.
Future interest that cuts short a prior interest.
Future interest must vest within lives in being plus 21 years.
Co-ownership with right of survivorship.
Co-ownership with no right of survivorship.
Co-ownership between spouses with survivorship.
Surviving co-owner takes deceased's share.
Right to use another's land for a specific purpose.
Easement benefiting an adjacent parcel of land.
Easement benefiting a person, not adjacent land.
Revocable permission to use another's land.
Promise binding successors in title to land.
Restriction enforceable in equity, binding successors.
Acquisition of title through long open occupation.
Apparent legal title that proves defective.
Security interest in real property to secure a debt.
Three-party security instrument transferring legal title.
Forced sale of property to satisfy a defaulted debt.
Claim against property securing payment of a debt.
Buyer for value without notice of competing claims.
Deed conveying property with title guarantees.
Deed transferring whatever interest the grantor has.
Government power to take private property for public use.
Government action depriving owner; requires compensation.
Regulation so restrictive it amounts to a taking.
Possessory interest in land for a defined term.
Item attached to real property and treated as part of it.
Transfer of personal property without transfer of ownership.
No one gives what they do not have.
Relationship where property is held for another's benefit.
Person who creates a trust by transferring property to it.
Person holding legal title to trust property as fiduciary.
Person who holds equitable title to trust property.
Trust the settlor may modify or terminate.
Trust the settlor cannot unilaterally change.
Trust for public benefit; favoured in equity.
Equitable remedy preventing unjust enrichment.
Heightened duty of loyalty and care to a beneficiary.
Fiduciary obligation to act solely in beneficiary's interest.
Standard requiring care, skill, and caution in trust investing.
Fiduciary acting on personal account; presumptively wrong.
Testamentary document directing property at death.
Person who makes a will.
Distribution of estate when there is no valid will.
By the roots; distribution by representation.
By the heads; distribution in equal shares.
Court-supervised administration of a deceased's estate.
Person named in a will to administer the estate.
Fair procedures owed before deprivation of liberty or property.
Protection of fundamental rights from arbitrary government action.
Right to fair procedures before adverse government action.
Government must treat similarly situated people alike.
Court oversight of legislative or executive action.
Allocation of governmental function across distinct branches.
Each branch limits the powers of the others.
Division of authority between national and state governments.
Federal law prevails over conflicting state law.
Federal law displacing state law in a regulated area.
Power to regulate interstate and foreign commerce.
Implicit limit on state regulation burdening interstate commerce.
Powers expressly granted to the federal government.
Powers reasonably inferred from enumerated ones.
Power to enact laws to execute enumerated powers.
Government bound by predictable, equally applied legal norms.
Constitution prevails over conflicting ordinary law.
Right to sue based on concrete injury, causation, redressability.
Loss of justiciability when controversy ends.
Doctrine requiring sufficient maturity of dispute for review.
Bar on review of issues committed to political branches.
Non-binding judicial opinion; generally prohibited federally.
Confidentiality protection for executive deliberations.
Directive from the executive with force of law.
Formal agreement between sovereign states.
Formal charge against a public official.
Right to express opinions without government suppression.
Right to practice or refrain from religion freely.
Prohibition of state establishment of religion.
Protection of religious practice from state interference.
Right to gather peaceably.
Right to join with others in expressive groups.
Right of mass communication free from prior restraint.
Most demanding standard of review; narrowly tailored, compelling.
Review requiring substantial relation to important interest.
Most deferential review; rationally related to legitimate interest.
Highest justification required under strict scrutiny.
Means closely fitted to the constitutional end.
Group basis triggering strict scrutiny under equal protection.
Government conduct subject to constitutional constraints.
Procedural minima: fair hearing and absence of bias.
Hear the other side; right to respond before adverse decision.
No one may be judge in their own cause.
Beyond the powers; act outside legal authority.
Means must be suitable, necessary, and fair to the end.
Reliance on official assurance protected against arbitrary change.
Decision so unreasonable no reasonable authority could make it.
Latitude granted to a decision-maker within legal limits.
Exercise of discretion beyond reasonable legal limits.
Improper rigid policy that forecloses individual assessment.
Obligation to articulate the basis of an administrative decision.
Agency promulgation of generally applicable regulations.
Agency decision in a particular case.
Court deference to reasonable agency interpretation of ambiguous statute.
Weak deference based on agency expertise and persuasiveness.
Deference to agency interpretation of its own regulations.
Standard for setting aside agency action without rational basis.
Standard of review requiring more than a scintilla of evidence.
Requirement to pursue agency review before judicial relief.
Public participation requirement in informal rulemaking.
Decisions taken solely on automated processing of data.
Claimed entitlement to reasons for an automated decision.
EU law prevails over conflicting national provisions.
EU provisions invocable directly in national courts.
Duty of national courts to interpret law consistently with EU directives.
National court request to CJEU for interpretation of EU law.
Decisions taken at the lowest competent level of governance.
EU acts only within powers granted by Member States.
Areas where only the EU may legislate.
Areas where both EU and Member States may legislate.
Internal-market freedom against trade barriers between Member States.
Right to provide services across Member State borders.
Right of EU citizens to take employment in any Member State.
Free flow of capital across Member State borders.
EU area without internal frontiers for goods, services, capital, persons.
EU alignment of national laws to enable single-market function.
Goods lawfully marketed in one Member State accepted in others.
EU bill of rights binding institutions and Member States.
Restriction on solely automated decision-making with significant effects.
EU regulation on artificial intelligence (2024).
AI system subject to heightened obligations under EU AI Act.
Law derived from formal agreements between states.
Law derived from general state practice accepted as law.
Peremptory norms permitting no derogation.
Legal accountability of states for internationally wrongful acts.
Exemption of diplomats from host-state jurisdiction.
Doctrine shielding states from foreign jurisdiction.
Bar on judging the validity of foreign state acts.
Right of peoples to determine political status freely.
Principle barring interference in another state's internal affairs.
UN Charter prohibition with self-defence exception.
Law governing the right to resort to armed force.
Law governing conduct during armed conflict.
Law protecting non-combatants in armed conflict.
Widespread or systematic attacks on civilian populations.
Acts intending to destroy a national, ethnic, racial, or religious group.
Serious violations of the laws of war.
Authority over certain crimes regardless of location or nationality.
Surrender of a person to another state for prosecution.
Protection granted to a refugee from persecution.
Prohibition of returning a person to face persecution.
Constitutionally entrenched basic individual entitlements.
Inherent worth grounding fundamental rights.
Protection against arbitrary deprivation of life.
Protection against arbitrary detention.
Procedural rights in determination of civil rights or criminal charges.
Protection of private life, family, home, and correspondence.
Right to hold opinions and impart information without interference.
Prohibition of unequal treatment without objective justification.
Absolute prohibition; jus cogens norm.
Absolute prohibition of slavery and servitude.
Peaceful enjoyment of possessions, subject to public-interest limits.
Access to education at primary and secondary levels.
Protection of family relationships from undue interference.
Latitude given to states in implementing convention rights.
1950 treaty protecting civil and political rights in Europe.
International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights.
International Covenant on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights.
1948 foundational human-rights declaration of the UN.
Legal entity separate from its owners.
Owners not personally liable beyond their investment.
Holding shareholders liable when entity is misused.
Owner of equity interest in a corporation.
Governing body responsible for corporate management.
Executive appointed by the board to manage operations.
Director must act in corporate interest, not self-interest.
Director must act with reasonable diligence and prudence.
Presumption protecting good-faith director decisions.
Trading securities on material non-public information.
Suit by shareholder on behalf of corporation against insiders.
Combination of two corporations into one entity.
Purchase of one corporation by another.
Public offer to purchase shares directly from shareholders.
Employment terminable by either party without cause.
Termination of employment in breach of contract or statute.
Negotiation of terms between employers and worker representatives.
Adverse employment treatment on protected grounds.
Unwelcome conduct of a sexual nature affecting employment.
Pervasive harassment altering conditions of employment.
Employment benefit conditioned on submission to advances.
Modification enabling disabled or religious employees to work.
Statutory floor on hourly compensation.
Premium pay for hours worked beyond standard threshold.
Worker organisation for collective representation.
Legally recognised partnership creating rights and duties.
Judicial dissolution of marriage.
Legal declaration that a marriage was never valid.
Legal authority over a child's care and upbringing.
Authority to make major decisions about the child.
Right to have the child physically present in one's home.
Court-ordered payments for a child's care.
Spousal support after separation or divorce.
Pre-marital contract on property and obligations.
Paramount consideration in decisions affecting a child.
Legal authority and duty regarding a child's welfare.
Exclusive rights in original works of authorship.
Exclusive rights in novel, useful, non-obvious inventions.
Source-identifying mark for goods or services.
Confidential information providing competitive advantage.
Limited use of copyrighted material without permission.
Unauthorised use violating an intellectual-property right.
Work based on or adapted from a pre-existing work.
Works free of copyright and freely usable.
Author's rights of attribution and integrity.
Defining language establishing the scope of patent protection.
Patentability barrier; non-obviousness is required.
Patentability requirement that invention not be previously disclosed.
Bar on creditor collection upon bankruptcy filing.
Release of debtor from personal liability for certain debts.
Pre-bankruptcy transfer favouring one creditor; recoverable.
Transfer made with intent to hinder creditors; voidable.
Creditor with collateral rights in debtor's property.
Order of payment among creditor classes.
Liquidation; non-exempt assets sold to pay creditors.
Reorganisation; debtor restructures while operating.
Individual reorganisation under a court-approved repayment plan.
Legal arrangement minimising tax liability.
Illegal non-payment or under-payment of tax.
Same income taxed in more than one jurisdiction.
Tax rate increasing with income.
Profit from sale of a capital asset.
Theory that law derives from objective moral truths.
Theory that law's existence depends on social facts, not merit.
Theory emphasising what judges actually do, not formal rules.
Movement treating law as politics in disguise.
Application of economic methods to legal analysis.
Critique of law's gendered assumptions and effects.
Examination of law's interaction with race and racial hierarchy.
Dworkinian view of law as principled coherence.
Hartian master rule identifying valid legal norms.
Indeterminacy at the borderlines of legal rules.
Case where settled rules do not dictate a single answer.
Dworkinian claim that hard cases have uniquely correct answers.
Determining the meaning of legal materials.
Reading provisions in light of their purpose.
Interpretation by ordinary meaning of statutory text.
Interpretation by original public meaning at enactment.
Interpretation guided by legislative purpose and context.
Kelsen's basic norm grounding legal validity.
Positivist claim that law and morality are separable.
Mid-20th-century exchange on law and morality.
Property of belonging to the legal system.
Justified authority to make and enforce binding decisions.
Habermasian action coordinated through reasoned consensus.
Justification appealing to shared public values.
Justice located in fair processes rather than outcomes.
Justice located in fair outcomes rather than processes.
Equal treatment regardless of relevant differences.
Equal outcomes accounting for relevant differences.
Predictability of legal rules and consequences.
Principle that law should not apply backwards in time.
Capacity of subjects to anticipate legal consequences.
Specific law overrides general law on same subject.
Later law overrides earlier inconsistent law.
Higher-ranked law overrides lower-ranked.
By that very fact; automatically as a consequence.
From the outset; effect retroactive to original time.
From now; effect prospective only.
In fact; existing in practice though not formally.
By law; formally recognised legal status.
From the beginning; void from the start.
On its face; sufficient at first appearance, absent rebuttal.
In good faith; honest intent without notice of defect.
In bad faith; dishonest intent.
Among other things.
By itself; intrinsically.
Trivial; below the threshold of legal concern.
Methods making AI decisions interpretable to humans.
Assignment of responsibility for algorithmic outcomes.
